deep_read.core_result_explanation: SAS won because its shot diet was materially cleaner and more efficient. The Spurs produced a 79.55% rim-or-three frequency, 17 made threes, 42.5% from three, and a 0.56 shot quality profile. OKC won the raw rebounding battle 51-47 and nearly matched offensive rebounding, but its shot mix was less efficient and more midrange-heavy, with only 58.82% rim-or-three frequency and 32.43% three-point shooting.
deep_read.rebounding_layer: OKC did not lose because of rebounding volume. OKC had 51 rebounds and 17 offensive boards, while SAS had 47 and 16. The hidden issue was rebound conversion: OKC second-chance volume did not translate into enough scoring separation because SAS maintained better shot efficiency and spacing on the other end.
deep_read.shot_distribution_layer: SAS created the superior math profile: 40 three-point attempts, 30 rim attempts, and only 18 combined short/mid/long midrange attempts. OKC had 25 short-midrange attempts and only 13 rim attempts, creating a lower-ceiling scoring profile in a Game 7 environment.
deep_read.turnover_layer: Both teams had live-ball turnover exposure, but OKC's 14 total turnovers and early-game giveaway pressure prevented sustained control. SAS committed 12 turnovers, but converted enough high-value shots to absorb them.
deep_read.foul_layer: Foul environment did not become a high-extension over engine. SAS had 18 fouls and OKC 16, with fouls drawn nearly balanced. This supports the halftime hook diagnosis: the 214.5 live over needed either cleaner late pace or foul extension, and neither fully materialized.

player_tracking_rebounding_notes.key_flag: Chet Holmgren's 20 rebound chances produced only 4 rebounds and a 22.2% adjusted chance rate, while Jaylin Williams and Cason Wallace were the stronger OKC rebound converters. This should influence future OKC frontcourt rebound prop calibration.

model_accuracy.SAS_moneyline: correct; model identified SAS win probability at 61.3% and user captured +118
model_accuracy.pregame_total: correct; over 211.5 won with final total 214
model_accuracy.halftime_total: miss; live over 214.5 lost by 0.5 due to Game 7 possession compression and insufficient foul extension

patches_to_roll_forward.rule: Reduce second-half pace and late-game total projection when elimination Game 7 live total rises above pregame by 3+ points and foul environment is balanced rather than accelerating.
Require median edge of 6+ points for correlated halftime over add-ons when pregame over is already held.
When Chet shows high rebound chances but low adjusted conversion, downgrade raw rebound chance-based overs and shift board share toward Jaylin/guards.
When SAS reaches 75%+ rim-or-three frequency with Wembanyama/Castle/Fox/Vassell active, boost upset viability and team total efficiency.
Do not overrate raw offensive rebound volume if shot quality and three-point efficiency gap favors opponent.
